Section Header: Piped In - Tumor Registry: Description of this Neoplasm
Field Label:
NAACCR Record Version
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: The NAACCR Layout version is necessary to communicate to the recipient of data in NAACCR form where the various items are found and how they are coded. It should be added to the record when the recorded is created. [NAACCR #50]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Cancer Sequence Number
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Code indicates the sequence of all reportable neoplasms over the lifetime of the person.  Each neoplasm is assigned a different number. Sequence Number 00 indicates that the person has had only one in situ or one malignant neoplasm as defined by the Federal reportable list (regardless of central registry reference date). Sequence Number 01 indicates the first of two or more reportable neoplasms, but 02 indicates the second of two or more reportable neoplasms, and so on. [NAACCR  # 380 (00-59, 99 = In situ/malignant   60-87 = non-malignant)]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Date of Diagnosis
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Date of initial diagnosis by a recognized medical practitioner for the tumor being reported whether clinically or microscopically confirmed. [NAACCR #390]

Field Label:
Date of Diagnosis In Days from DOB (Interval)
Calculated Field
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: datediff([birth_date],[dx_date_tr],"d","mdy",true)
Field Note: integer

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Age at Diagnosis
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Age of the patient at diagnosis in complete years. Different tumors for the same patient may have different values. [NAACCR #230]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Diagnostic Confirmation

PIPED IN Only
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Positive histology | 2, Positive cytology | 3, Positive histology (plus positive immunophenotyping AND/OR positive genetic studies) [Hematopoietic and Lymphoid Neoplasms only] | 4, Positive microscopic confirmation, method not specified | 5, Positive laboratory test/marker study | 6, Direct visualization without microscopic confirmation | 7, Radiology and other imaging techniques without microscopic confirmation | 8, Clinical diagnosis only (other than 5, 6, or 7) | 9, Unknown whether or not microscopically confirmed; death certificate only
Field Note: Code for the best method of diagnostic confirmation of the cancer being reported at any time in the patient's history. Diagnostic confirmation is useful to calculate rates based on microscopically confirmed cancers. Full incidence calculations must also include tumors that are only confirmed clinically. The percentage of tumors that not micropscopically confirmed is an indication of whether case finding is including sources outside of pathology reports. [NAACCR #490]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Primary Site Code (ICD-O-3)
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Code for the primary site of the tumor being reported using either ICD-O-2 or ICD-O-3. C followed by 3 digits, no special characters, no embedded blanks  [NAACCR #400]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Histologic Type (ICD-O-3)
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Codes for the histologic type of the tumor being reported using ICD-O-3. 8000-9989, Refer to ICD-O-3 [NAACCR #522]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Behavior Code (ICD-O-3)
PIPED IN Only
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 0, Benign (Reportable for intracranial and CNS sites only) | 1, Uncertain whether benign or malignant, borderline malignancy, low malignant potential, and uncertain malignant potential (Reportable for intracranial and CNS sites only) | 2, Carcinoma in situ; intraepithelial; noninfiltrating; non-invasive (carcinoma) | 3, Malignant, primary site (invasive)
Field Note: Code for the behavior of the tumor being reported using ICD-O-3. 0-3, Refer to ICD-O-3 [NAACCR #523]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Laterality

PIPED IN Only
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 0, Not a paired site | 1, Right: origin of primary | 2, Left: origin of primary | 3, Only one side involved, right or left origin unspecified | 4, Bilateral involvement at time of diagnosis, lateral origin unknown for a single primary; or both ovaries involved simultaneously, single histology; bilateral retinoblastomas; bilateral Wilms tumors | 5, Paired site: midline tumor (effective with 01/01/2010 dx) | 9, Paired site, but no information concerning laterality
Field Note: Code for the side of a paired organ, or the side of the body on which the reportable tumor originated. This applies to the primary site only. [NAACCR #410]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Grade Clinical
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This data item records the grade of a solid primary tumor before any treatment (surgical resection or initiation of any treatment including neoadjuvant). Grade is a measure of the aggressiveness of the tumor. Grade and cell type are important prognostic indicators for many cancers. For those cases that are eligible AJCC staging, the recommended grading system is specified in the AJCC Chapter. The AJCC Chapter-specific grading systems (codes 1-5) take priority over the generic grade definitions (codes A-E, L, H, 9). For those cases that are not eligible for AJCC staging, if the recommended grading system is not documented, the generic grade definitions would apply. [NAACCR #3843]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Grade Post Therapy Clin (yc)
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This data item records the grade of a solid primary tumor that has been microscopically sampled following neoadjuvant therapy or primary systemic/radiation therapy. If AJCC staging is being assigned, the tumor must have met the neoadjuvant therapy or primary systemic/radiation therapy requirements in the AJCC manual or according to national treatment guidelines.  Record the highest grade documented from the microscopically sampled specimen of the primary site following neoadjuvant therapy or primary systemic/radiation therapy.  For cases diagnosed January 1, 2020, and later, this data item, along with Grade Clinical, Grade Pathological, and Grade Post Therapy Path (yp), replaces NAACCR Data Item Grade {440} as well as SSF's for cancer sites with alternative grading systems (e.g., breast {Bloom-Richardson}, prostate {Gleason}) [NAACCR #1068]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Grade Pathological
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This data item records the grade of a solid primary tumor that has been resected and for which no neoadjuvant therapy was administered. If AJCC staging is being assigned, the tumor must have met the surgical resection requirements in the AJCC manual. This may include the grade from the clinical workup.  Record the highest grade documented from any microscopic specimen of the primary site whether from the clinical workup or the surgical resection.  For cases diagnosed January 1, 2018, and later, this data item, along with Grade Clinical and Grade Post-Neoadjuvant, replaces NAACCR Data Item Grade {440} as well as SSF's for cancer sites with alternative grading systems (e.g., breast {Bloom-Richardson}, prostate {Gleason}). Grade is a measure of the aggressiveness of the tumor. Grade and cell type are important prognostic indicators for many cancers. For some sites, grade is required to assign the pathological stage group.  For those cases that are eligible AJCC staging, the recommended grading system is specified in the AJCC Chapter. The AJCC Chapter-specific grading systems (codes 1-5) take priority over the generic grade definitions (codes A-E, L, H, 9). For those cases that are not eligible for AJCC staging, if the recommended grading system is not documented, the generic grade definitions would apply [NAACCR #3844]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Grade Post Therapy Path (yp)
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This data item records the grade of a solid primary tumor that has been resected following neoadjuvant therapy. If AJCC staging is being assigned, the tumor must have met the surgical resection requirements in the AJCC manual.  Record the highest grade documented from the surgical treatment resection specimen of the primary site following neoadjuvant therapy.  For cases diagnosed January 1, 2018, and later, this data item, along with Grade Clinical and Grade Pathological, replaces NAACCR Data Item Grade {440} as well as SSF's for cancer sites with alternative grading systems (e.g., breast {Bloom-Richardson}, prostate {Gleason}). Grade is a measure of the aggressiveness of the tumor. Grade and cell type are important prognostic indicators for many cancers. For some sites, grade is required to assign the post-neoadjuvant stage group.  For those cases that are eligible AJCC staging, the recommended grading system is specified in the AJCC Chapter. The AJCC Chapter-specific grading systems (codes 1-5) take priority over the generic grade definitions (codes A-E, L, H, 9). For those cases that are not eligible for AJCC staging, if the recommended grading system is not documented, the generic grade definitions would apply. [NAACCR #3845]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Derived Summary Grade 2018
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This data item will be derived from the following existing fields:  3843: Grade Clinical 3844: Grade Pathological Note: Prior to 2018, grade was collected prior to neoadjuvant therapy, which is why Grade Post-therapy Clin (yc) and Grade Post-Therapy (yp) are not part of the calculation This new data item will be applied to cases for 2018+ (would not be applicable for cases diagnosed prior to 2018). This would not be a conversion but deriving a new data item based on information already in the cancer registry systems. Once new cases are entered, then the Derived Summary Grade will be derived. [NAACCR #1975]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Tumor Size Summary
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This data item records the most accurate measurement of a solid primary tumor, usually measured on the surgical resection specimen. [NAACCR #756]

Section Header: Piped In - Tumor Registry: Historical Fields
Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - AJCC Edition Number
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: A code that indicates the edition of the AJCC manual used to stage the case. This applies to the manually coded AJCC fields. It does not apply to the Derived AJCC T, N, M and AJCC Stage Group fields {2940, 2960, 2980, and 3000}. TNM codes have changed over time and conversion is not always simple. Therefore, a case-specific indicator is needed to allow grouping of cases for comparison. [NAACCR #1060]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - Grade
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Code for the grade or degree of differentiation of the reportable tumor. For lymphomas and leukemias, field also is used to indicate T-, B-, Null-, or NK-cell origin. [NAACCR #440]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - SEER Summary Stage
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Code for summary stage at the initial diagnosis or treatment of the reportable tumor. For hospital registries, CoC requires its use in the absence of a defined AJCC classification. For site-specific definitions of categories, see SEER Summary Staging Manual 2000.  Summary stage should include all information available through completion of surgery(ies) in the first course of treatment or within 4 months of diagnosis in the absence of disease progression, whichever is longer. [NAACCR #759]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - Clinical T
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the clinical tumor (T) as defined by AJCC and recorded by the physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #940]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - Clinical N
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the clinical nodes (N) as defined by AJCC and recorded by the physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #950]

Field Label:

PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the clinical metastases (M) as defined by AJCC and recorded by the physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #960]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - Clinical Stage Group
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the clinical stage group as defined by AJCC and recorded by the physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #970]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - Pathologic T
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the pathologic tumor (T) as defined by AJCC and recorded by the physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #880]

Tumor Registry (Historical) - Pathologic N
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the pathologic nodes (N) as defined by AJCC and recorded by physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #890]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry (Historical) - Pathologic M
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the pathologic metastases (M) as defined by AJCC and recorded by the physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #900]

Tumor Registry (Historical) - Pathologic Stage Group
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Detailed site-specific codes for the pathologic stage group as defined by AJCC and recorded by the physician.  Pathologic and clinical stage data are given three separate areas in the NAACCR Data Exchange Record Layout. [NAACCR #910]

Section Header: Piped In - Tumor Registry: Stage of Disease at Diagnosis
Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Extent of Disease Primary Tumor
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: EOD Primary Tumor is part of the EOD 2018 data collection system and is used to classify contiguous growth (extension) of the primary tumor within the organ of origin or its direct extension into neighboring organs. See also EOD Regional Nodes {774} and EOD Mets {776}. Effective for cases diagnosed 1/1/2018+. [NAACCR #772]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Extent of Disease Regional Nodes
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: EOD Regional Nodes is part of the EOD 2018 data collection system and is used to classify the regional lymph nodes involved with cancer at the time of diagnosis. See also EOD Primary Tumor {772} and EOD Mets {776}. Effective for cases diagnosed 1/1/2018+ [NAACCR #774]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Extent of Disease Metastases
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: EOD Mets is part of the EOD 2018 data collection system and is used to classify the distant site(s) of metastatic involvement at time of diagnosis. See also EOD Primary Tumor {772} and EOD Regional Nodes {774}. Effective for cases diagnosed 1/1/2018+ [NAACCR #776]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Summary Stage 2018
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This item stores the directly assigned Summary Stage 2018. Effective for cases diagnosed 1/1/2018+ [NAACCR #764]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Derived Summary Stage 2018
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Derived Summary Stage 2018 is derived using the EOD data collection system (EOD Primary Tumor {772}, EOD Regional Nodes {774} and EOD Mets {776}) algorithm. Other data items may be included in the derivation process. Effective for cases diagnosed 1/1/2018+ [NAACCR #762]

Section Header: Piped In - Tumor Registry: Stage-related Data Items
Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Lymphovascular Invasion
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Lymphovascular invasion (LVI) indicates the presence or absence of tumor cells in lymphatic channels (not lymph nodes) or blood vessels within the primary tumor as noted microscopically by the pathologist. LVI includes lymphatic invasion, vascular invasion, and lymphovascular invasion. [NAACCR #1182]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Mets at Diagnosis: Bone
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field identifies whether bone is an involved metastatic site. The six Mets at Dx-Metastatic Sites fields provide information on specific metastatic sites for data analysis. [NAACCR #1112]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Mets at Diagnosis: Brain
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field identifies whether brain is an involved metastatic site. The six Mets at Dx-Metastatic Sites fields provide information on specific metastatic sites for data analysis. [NAACCR #1113]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Mets at Diagnosis: Liver
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field identifies whether liver is an involved metastatic site. The six Mets at Dx-Metastatic Sites fields provide information on specific metastatic sites for data analysis. [NAACCR #1115]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Mets at Diagnosis: Lung
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field identifies whether lung is an involved metastatic site. The six Mets at Dx-Metastatic Sites fields provide information on specific metastatic sites for data analysis. [NAACCR #1116]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Mets at Diagnosis: Distant Lymph Node(s)
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field identifies whether distant lymph node(s) are an involved metastatic site. The six Mets at Dx-Metastatic Sites fields provide information on specific metastatic sites for data analysis. [NAACCR #1114]

Field Label:
Tumor Registry - Mets at Diagnosis--Other
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: The six Mets at Dx-Metastatic Sites fields provide information on metastases for data analysis. This field identifies any type of distant involvement not captured in the Mets at Dx-Bone {1112}, Mets at Dx-Brain {1113}, Mets at Dx-Liver {1115}, Mets at Dx-Lung {1116}, and Mets at Dx-Distant LN {1114} fields. It includes involvement of other specific sites and more generalized metastases such as carcinomatosis. Some examples include but are not limited to the adrenal gland, bone marrow, pleura, malignant pleural effusion, peritoneum, and skin. [NAACCR #1117]

https://seer.cancer.gov/manuals/2025/SPCSM_2025_MainDoc.pdf
Field Note: Date of initial diagnosis by a recognized medical practitioner for the tumor being reported whether clinically or microscopically confirmed. [NAACCR #390]

Year of Diagnosis

Field Note: Calculated field

DOB to Diagnosis Date (Interval in Days)

Field Note: Calculated field

Age at Diagnosis (Year)
Calculated Field

Field Note: Calculated field

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Internal Institution | 2, External Institution

Field Label: What is the zip code of the facility that diagnosed the cancer?
Field Note: Provide five digit zip code for location of facility where patient was diagnosed

Field Label: Diagnostic Confirmation
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Positive histology | 2, Positive cytology | 3, Positive histology (plus positive immunophenotyping AND/OR positive genetic studies) [Hematopoietic and Lymphoid Neoplasms only] | 4, Positive microscopic confirmation, method not specified | 5, Positive laboratory test/marker study | 6, Direct visualization without microscopic confirmation | 7, Radiology and other imaging techniques without microscopic confirmation | 8, Clinical diagnosis only (other than 5, 6, or 7) | 9, Unknown whether or not microscopically confirmed; death certificate only
Field Note: Code for the best method of diagnostic confirmation of the cancer being reported at any time in the patient's history. Diagnostic confirmation is useful to calculate rates based on microscopically confirmed cancers. Full incidence calculations must also include tumors that are only confirmed clinically. The percentage of tumors that not micropscopically confirmed is an indication of whether case finding is including sources outside of pathology reports. [NAACCR #490]

Field Note: Code for the grade or degree of differentiation of the reportable tumor. For lymphomas and leukemias, field also is used to indicate T-, B-, Null-, or NK-cell origin. (NAACCR Item #: 440) This data item records the grade of a solid primary tumor before any treatment (surgical resection or initiation of any treatment including neoadjuvant). Grade is a measure of the aggressiveness of the tumor. Grade and cell type are important prognostic indicators for many cancers. For those cases that are eligible AJCC staging, the recommended grading system is specified in the AJCC Chapter. The AJCC Chapter-specific grading systems (codes 1-5) take priority over the generic grade definitions (codes A-E, L, H, 9). For those cases that are not eligible for AJCC staging, if the recommended grading system is not documented, the generic grade definitions would apply. (NAACCR Item #: 3843 - dx > 2018) [NAACCR #440 and #3843]

Field Note: Federation Internationale de Gynecologie et d'Obstetrique (FIGO) is a staging system for female reproductive cancers. FIGO is a worldwide organization of obstetricians and gynecologists who maintain the international staging systems for female genital organs. The FIGO staging system has been adapted into the AJCC staging manual. FIGO uses Roman numerals and subscripts to define a stage. Do not confuse FIGO stage with FIGO grade. FIGO stage is a Registry Data Collection Variable in AJCC for the female genital cancers. [NAACCR #3836]

Field Label: Ovarian - Residual Tumor Volume Post Cytoreduction
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 00, No gross residual tumor nodules | 50, Residual tumor nodule(s) 1 centimeter (cm) or less | 60, Residual tumor nodule(s) greater than 1 cm | 70, Macroscopic residual tumor, size not stated | 80, Procedure described as optimal debulking and size of residual tumor nodule(s) not given | 97, No cytoreductive surgery performed, Non-invasive neoplasm (behavior /2) | 98, Not applicable: Information not collected for this case | 99, Not documented in medical record; Residual tumor status after cytoreductive surgery not assessed or unknown if assessed
Field Note: Gross residual tumor after primary cytoreductive surgery is a prognostic factor for ovarian cancer and residual tumor volume after cytoreductive surgery is a prognostic factor for late-stage ovarian cancers. The amount of ovarian tumor and the location of tumor remaining in the patient after initial ovarian or peritoneal cancer surgery are the most important prognostic factors for advanced disease. Residual Tumor Volume Post Cytoreduction is a Registry Data Collection Variable listed in AJCC. [NAACCR #3921]

Field Label: Brain/CNS - Brain Primary Tumor Location
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Pons | 2, Subsite other than Pons | 8, Not Applicable: Information not collected for this case | 9, Brain Stem, NOS; Unknown subsite of Brain Stem | 10, Primary site is not the Brain Stem (C717)
Field Note: The Pons and other subsites of the Brain Stem have the same ICD-O topography code (C717), which is for subsites of the Brain Stem. Clinically, information regarding the Pons is very important, especially for pediatric cases. A data item is needed to distinguish between the Pons and all other subsites within the Brain Stem (C717). Code the site in which the tumor arose. Review of radiology reports and physician's notes are needed to determine the appropriate code. Needle biopsies and surgery are usually too dangerous for these types of tumors, so the best information will be available from radiology reports. [NAACCR #3964]

Field Label: Has the patient undergone any radiographic imaging at any point during their treatment?

Field Label: At which facility was the imaging performed?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Internal Institution | 2, External Institution

Field Label: Date of Radiology Imaging Procedure

Calculated Field

Field Label: Date of Reference Imaging  (previous imaging of this cancer)

Date of Reference Imaging in Days from DOB (Interval)
Calculated Field

Field Label: What location(s) of the body were imaged?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Brain | 2, Head and Neck | 3, Spine | 4, Chest/Thorax | 5, Abdomen | 6, Pelvis | 7, Extremities (upper/arms) | 8, Extremities (lower/arms) | 9, Whole Body

Field Label: Does the report indicate evidence of disease?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, The report states that there is evidence of disease | 2, The report states that there is no evidence of disease | 3, The report does not mention evidence of disease or is indeterminate regarding evidence of disease
Field Note: Record whether the impression section of the radiology report indicates the presence or absence of disease based on the findings of the imaging procedure. Choose the appropriate option based on the report's statement regarding evidence of disease

Field Label: Cancer 1: Provide the radiologist's impression of patient's cancer status

Field Note: Circumferential or Radial Resection Margin, the distance in millimeters between the leading edge of the tumor and the surgically dissected margin as recorded on the pathology report, is a prognostic indicator for colon and rectal cancer. Field Note: Kidney Tumor Extension pertains to the pathologically confirmed invasion of the tumor beyond the fibrous capsule in which the kidney is enclosed. [NAACCR #3864]

Field Label: Kidney - Major Vein Involvement
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 0, Major vein involvement not present/not identified | 1, Renal vein or its segmental branches | 2, Inferior vena cava (IVC) | 3, Major vein invasion, NOS | 4, Any combination of codes 1-3 | 8, Not applicable: Information not collected for this case | 9, Not documented in medical record; Vein involvement not assessed or unknown if assessed; No surgical resection of primary site is performed
Field Note: This data item records information about the presence and level of involvement of specific major blood vessels. Do not code microscopically identified involvement of small unnamed blood vessels within the kidney; this information is coded in the field Lymph-Vascular Invasion (LVI). The tumor may be described as a thrombus, a cluster of tumor cells presents in the center of the vein but not attached to the wall of the vein. Tumor spread may resemble mud extruding along the inside of a pipe. Direct tumor invasion of the wall of the inferior vena cava is not coded in this field. Record the code that best describes involvement of the renal vein and/or inferior vena cava (IVC) as described in the pathology report. Do not include clinical findings in this field. Involvement of major veins for Kidney is a Registry Data Collection Variable in AJCC. [NAACCR #3886]

Section Header:
Curated - Tumor Sample Information

***PLEASE READ BEFORE STARTING THIS SECTION***

This section collects molecular information for GENIE sequenced samples as well as non-GENIE samples that may not have yet been submitted to the GENIE registry.

 

For Exisiting GENIE patients/samples: Sites will be provided with GENIE patient IDs, as well as associated GENIE sample IDs.

For new patients/samples not yet in the GENIE registry: Utilize the sample IDs provided by your site's GENIE uplaoder team.

Field Label: Has the patient been hospitalized at any point following initial cancer diagnosis?
Field Note: [NAACCR # 605]

Section Header:
Hospitalizations Information

 

 

        For Chordoma Study: 

    •    Only record in-patient hospitalizations, do not record ER visits
    •    Only review hospitalizations occurring at GENIE participating site
    •    Do not review "outside records" or faxed records of admissions / hospitalizations at external facilities
Field Label: At which type of facility was patient hospitalized?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Internal Institution | 2, External Institution | 3, Both Internal and External Institutions
Field Note: Note whether the hospitalization occurred at the GENIE participating institution (Internal Institution), elsewhere (External Institution), or both (hospital transfer)

Field Label: Hospitalization Start Date
Field Note: Document the exact calendar date when the patient was admitted to the hospital. This should be the date the patient was officially admitted for treatment, observation, or any other medical reasons

Field Label:
Hospitalization Start in Days from DOB (Interval)
Calculated Field
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: datediff([birth_date],[hosp_start_date],"d","mdy",true)
Field Note: Calculated field

Field Label: Hospitalization End Date
Field Note: Document the exact calendar date when the patient was discharged or formally released from the hospital. This should be the date the patient completed their hospital stay, whether due to recovery, treatment completion, or another reason.

Field Label:
Hospitalization End in Days from DOB (Interval)
Calculated Field
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: datediff([birth_date],[hosp_end_date],"d","mdy",true)
Field Note: Calculated field

Field Label: Reason for Hospitalization
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Illness | 2, Cancer Treatment | 3, Treatment Related Event | 4, Monitoring | 5, Other
Field Note: Indicate the reason(s) for the patient's hospitalization. Select the option(s) that best describes the cause of the hospital admission

Field Label: Reason for hospitalization (Other) - Please specify
Field Note: If "Other" is selected as the reason for hospitalization, please provide a detailed explanation for the patient's admission. This could include specific medical conditions, complications, or circumstances that do not fall under "Illness" or "Treatment." Ensure the description clearly reflects the reason for the hospitalization based on the patient's medical records.

Field Label: Was hospitalization due to a treatment toxicity?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unknown
Field Note: Indicate whether the patient's hospitalization was caused by a toxicity or adverse reaction related to cancer treatment.

Field Label:
If grade of toxicity was mentioned, what was the grade?

For more details on coding see:

https://dctd.cancer.gov/research/ctep-trials/for-sites/adverse-events/ctcae-v6.pdf
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Grade 1 | 2, Grade 2 | 3, Grade 3 | 4, Grade 4 | 5, Grade 5 | 6, Toxicity Grade Not Specified
Field Note: Indicate the severity of the treatment-related toxicity if it was documented in the patient's medical records. Select the option that best describes the grade of toxicity, based on the Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE)

Field Label:
Specimen Collection Date
PIPED IN Only

Field Label:
Date of Specimen Collection in Days from DOB (Interval)
Calculated Field
Field Note: Calculated field

Field Label:
Laboratory Test - Name
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Describes the type of laboratory test performed on the specimen (CBC, CMP, TSH, etc.)

Field Label:
Laboratory Test - LOINC Code
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: Captures the associated LOINC code for laboratory test

Field Label:
Laboratory Test - Result
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field captures the outcome or result (value) of the laboratory test conducted on the specimen.

Field Label:
Laboratory Test - Unit
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field captures the unit of measurement for the result of the laboratory test. The unit will be automatically populated based on the specific test performed (e.g., g/dL, IU/L, ng/mL).

Field Label:
Laboratory Test - Lower Range Normal
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field captures the lower reference range for the laboratory test result. The value will be automatically populated based on the specific test performed. This range indicates the minimum normal value for the test, provided by the laboratory.

Field Label:
Laboratory Test - Upper Range Normal
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field captures the upper reference range for the laboratory test result. The value will be automatically populated based on the specific test performed. This range indicates the minimum normal value for the test, provided by the laboratory.

Field Label:
Laboratory Test - Result Comparison to Range
PIPED IN Only
Field Note: This field captures the status of the laboratory test result in relation to its reference range. The value will be automatically populated based on the result of the test, with potential values including: Low, In Range, High, Out of Range
